summary: Retest of iteration 7 CRITICAL fix. Verified TWO newly-accessible TPM surfaces PLUS full regression on CFO read-only and 2-stage top-up approval. (a) TpmDashboard now renders <ProjectsTable/> below charts — projects-table + 4 row-toggle-* + expandable phase-drawer confirmed. (b) PhaseWorkspace fully wired to TpmTaskLogDialog + TopupRequestDialog — btn-add-task opens task-log-dialog, submit creates ws-log-* entry with ws-log-edit-*/ws-log-delete-* icons; btn-raise-topup-phase opens topup-dialog with phase pre-selected (p2). All 10 review-request scenarios pass. Console clean (0 errors); only 8 pre-existing Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on dashboard render (non-blocking). Hydration warning about <span> in <option> from iteration 7 also gone in this run.

issues: Recharts warning 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' repeats ~8x on load — ResponsiveContainer parent sizing (pre-existing, non-blocking, does not affect UX).

action_items: Nice-to-have: Address Recharts ResponsiveContainer width(-1)/height(-1) warnings by giving parent a fixed min-height or explicit sizing to eliminate console noise.
critical_code_review_comments: TpmDashboard.jsx line 265 correctly imports & renders <ProjectsTable/> below the charts grid — TPM now has drawer access.
PhaseWorkspace.jsx correctly wires TpmTaskLogDialog + TopupRequestDialog with defaultPhaseId={phase.id}; ws-log-edit-* / ws-log-delete-* respect isTaskEditable 24h window.
TopupRequestDialog label is now computed as a plain string (no <span> child inside <option>) — hydration warning resolved.

TopupRequestDetail.jsx testids are input-approved-amt / input-approved-slider / input-comment / btn-approve / btn-partial / btn-reject (not partial-amount/partial-submit) — verified 2-stage flow works correctly with $48k -> $50k baseline update.

Non-blocking: PhaseWorkspace.jsx is ~449 lines — still under 700-line threshold but approaching; consider splitting StatCard + tpm-logs-panel + tasks-table into subcomponents when adding more features.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 10/10 review test items pass (TPM ProjectsTable on dashboard, TPM drawer log/topup, PhaseWorkspace add-task + raise-topup + 24h edit lock, 2-stage CTO->CFO approval with baseline bump $48k->$50k utilization 85%->82%, CFO strict read-only on /projects + ProjectDetail + PhaseWorkspace + Dashboard drawer).

test_credentials: Quick-login cards on /login: quick-login-tpm (Arjun Mehta), quick-login-cto (Vikram Kumar), quick-login-cfo (Priya Kapoor). No password.
seed_data_creation: None — used AppContext seedTopupRequests (tur-seed-1 pending-cto) present per Playwright context. Consumed tur-seed-1 in Stage-1 partial ($2000 by CTO) -> Stage-2 approve (by CFO) flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Correct testids on TopupRequestDetail.jsx are input-approved-amt (number input), input-approved-slider (Radix slider), input-comment (textarea), btn-approve / btn-partial / btn-reject. Phase IDs in mockProjects.js are p1/p2/p3/p4 (NOT phase-1). CFO can navigate to any /projects/:id/phase/:phaseId — PhaseWorkspace falls back to PROJECTS static data via visibleProjects.find || PROJECTS.find. Playwright fresh context: seeded top-ups reset — use localStorage.removeItem('ethara.session.v1') to switch roles while keeping top-up state within one script.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration 7 CRITICAL gap (TPM cannot reach phase-drawer) has been fully fixed. Two independent code paths now expose the daily task log + phase-scoped top-up: (1) TpmDashboard imports ProjectsTable and renders <ProjectsTable/> after the charts grid, giving TPM the same drawer/log/topup flow non-TPM roles already had; (2) PhaseWorkspace now uses TpmTaskLogDialog (openNewLog on btn-add-task and btn-log-task-inline) and TopupRequestDialog (btn-raise-topup-phase with defaultPhaseId={phase.id}), so TPM can log/top-up directly from /projects/:id/phase/:phaseId. Verified end-to-end via playwright: task-log-dialog submit -> toast success -> ws-log-edit-<id>/ws-log-delete-<id> rendered in tpm-logs-panel (within 24h), and topup submit toast 'Top-up request submitted, Talos · Phase 1 · $3,000 - routed to CTO'. Also verified 2-stage approval: CTO partial $2000 on tur-seed-1 -> CFO Stage-2 approve -> Crowley Generation approvedBudget $48.0k -> $50.0k (utilization 85% -> 82%, effective-with-12%-buffer $53.8k -> $56.0k). No open issues.
